trivia_bullets.text: Originally printed in Dominaria (DOM-218, April 27, 2018). Commander Legends (CMR-316, November 20, 2020) is its second printing. Also in Game Night (2018), Game Night 2019, and Game Night: Free-for-All (2022). All five printings use the same Grzegorz Rutkowski art.

Artist Grzegorz Rutkowski is a prolific Polish fantasy illustrator with over 90 Magic card credits including Drakuseth Maw of Flames, Glorybringer, and God-Eternal Oketra. Howling Golem is a comparatively modest subject in his portfolio — a stone construct in a grassy field vs. the dragons and gods he is better known for.
sources: ArtStation: Greg Rutkowski profile, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Reprinted in all three Game Night products (2018, 2019, 2022) — Wizards's casual 3-5 player introductory product. The symmetrical draw trigger (every attacking or blocking trigger draws for all players) makes it a natural fit for casual multiplayer tables.

"It wails of buried riches and the souls lost seeking them." The flavor frames the golem's howl as grief for those who died seeking treasure — lament rather than aggression, consistent with the art's mournful open-mouthed pose in a desolate field.

EDHREC rank 6,302 — niche. As a 2/3 for {3} with symmetrical draw on attack or block, serves primarily in group-hug or wheel-synergy Commander builds. Symmetrical nature is a competitive drawback but a casual-multiplayer attraction.

related_cards.name: Howling Mine
related_cards.note: Canonical symmetrical draw artifact; frequently compared to Howling Golem as creature-based variant

related_cards.name: Temple Bell
related_cards.note: Another symmetrical draw artifact grouped with Howling Mine in group-hug lists

related_cards.name: Lore Broker
related_cards.note: Older creature with symmetrical draw-discard trigger; functional ancestor of combat-triggered draw design
